seed_knowledge.title: Initialize project with lockfile first
seed_knowledge.description: Before writing application code, initialize dependency management system and commit verified lockfile. This ensures reproducible builds from first commit.
verification_steps: 1. Run package manager init command with explicit lock flag
2. Verify lockfile hash matches official registry checksums
3. Commit only lockfile and manifest before any source code
anti_patterns: Committing application code before lockfile exists
Using loose version ranges in initial manifest

seed_knowledge.title: Minimum viable logging first
seed_knowledge.description: Implement structured logging before any business logic. All entry points must log execution context before processing input.
verification_steps: 1. Configure structured logger with trace ID propagation
2. Verify every public method logs entry and exit
3. No console.log/print statements allowed in production code
anti_patterns: Adding logging after bugs are discovered

seed_knowledge.title: Solo capability validation first
seed_knowledge.description: Every module must operate correctly with zero external dependencies before integrating external services. All external calls are optional boosters only.
verification_steps: 1. Run module tests with all external endpoints mocked
2. Verify core functionality passes 100% of tests offline
3. External integration only enhances capability not enables it
anti_patterns: Core functionality requires external API to work
Failing open when external services are unavailable
Tight coupling to external provider interfaces

seed_knowledge.title: Input validation at trust boundary
seed_knowledge.description: All external input must be validated at the system boundary before any internal processing occurs.
verification_steps: 1. Define schema for every external input
2. Reject invalid input before any logic runs
3. No partial processing of invalid input

seed_knowledge.title: Default feature flags off
seed_knowledge.description: All new features are deployed disabled by default. Enable only after verification in production environment.
verification_steps: 1. Every feature has independent kill switch
2. Default state in code is disabled
3. Feature enablement is runtime configurable without deploy
anti_patterns: Requiring deploy to disable a feature

seed_knowledge.title: Smallest correct fix principle
seed_knowledge.description: Always implement the minimal possible change that correctly resolves the error. No refactoring, improvements or cleanup in bug fix commits.
verification_steps: 1. Identify root cause before writing any code
2. Measure diff size before commit
3. Verify no unrelated lines are modified
anti_patterns: Adding new features during bug resolution
Rewriting entire modules to fix one error

seed_knowledge.title: Reproduce error before fixing
seed_knowledge.description: Create automated failing test case that reproduces the exact error before implementing any fix.
verification_steps: 1. Write test case that reliably fails with reported error
2. Verify test passes after fix is applied
3. Add test case to permanent test suite

seed_knowledge.title: Never silently ignore errors
seed_knowledge.description: Every caught error must be either handled correctly, logged with full context, or propagated upstream. No empty catch blocks.
verification_steps: 1. Audit all exception handlers
2. Verify every catch block has explicit action
3. No generic exception swallowing
anti_patterns: Logging only error message without stack trace

seed_knowledge.title: Check upstream first
seed_knowledge.description: Before modifying code, verify error is not caused by upstream dependency, infrastructure or configuration change.
verification_steps: 1. Check dependency version changes
2. Verify infrastructure status metrics
3. Compare working and non-working environment configurations
anti_patterns: Modifying application code for infrastructure issues
Rolling back application without verifying root cause

seed_knowledge.title: Validate fix impact radius
seed_knowledge.description: Before deploying fix, map all code paths that will be affected by the change and verify test coverage for each path.
verification_steps: 1. Generate call graph for modified functions
2. Verify test coverage for every affected code path
3. Run full regression test suite before deploy
anti_patterns: Testing only the reported error case

seed_knowledge.title: Cloud first stateless design
seed_knowledge.description: All services must be designed stateless first. Any state must be stored in managed external storage services.
verification_steps: 1. No in-memory state across requests
2. Any instance can serve any request
3. Terminating any instance causes zero data loss
anti_patterns: Storing session state in process memory
Local disk storage for application data

seed_knowledge.title: Single responsibility per deployment unit
seed_knowledge.description: Each deployable service must have exactly one primary responsibility. Split services when they acquire additional responsibilities.
verification_steps: 1. Service can be described in one sentence without conjunctions
2. Service has single health check endpoint
3. Service exposes one primary interface
anti_patterns: God services handling multiple unrelated domains
Services with multiple independent health checks
Services that act as client and server for unrelated flows

seed_knowledge.title: Explicit dependencies only
seed_knowledge.description: All service dependencies must be explicitly declared and injected. No implicit dependencies on environmental side effects.
verification_steps: 1. All external dependencies are passed as constructor arguments
2. No global variables or singletons
3. All dependencies can be mocked for testing

seed_knowledge.title: Fail closed by default
seed_knowledge.description: All security controls, access checks and validation gates default to denying access. Explicit allow list only.
verification_steps: 1. Default return value for all checks is deny
2. All exceptions in check logic result in deny
3. Only explicitly allowed operations are permitted

seed_knowledge.title: Eventual consistency explicit
seed_knowledge.description: All operations that do not require immediate consistency must be explicitly designed for eventual consistency.
verification_steps: 1. Document consistency requirement for every operation
2. No read after write expectations unless explicitly required
3. Retry logic handles intermediate inconsistent states
anti_patterns: Assuming immediate consistency for all operations

seed_knowledge.title: Secrets never logged
seed_knowledge.description: All secrets, credentials and keys must be marked as sensitive and automatically redacted from all logs, traces and error messages.
verification_steps: 1. Sensitive fields have dedicated type marking
2. Automatic redaction at logging layer
3. No secrets appear in any crash dump or stack trace
anti_patterns: Logging full exception messages with secrets
Passing secrets as command line arguments

seed_knowledge.title: Minimum required permissions
seed_knowledge.description: All service accounts, users and processes are granted the absolute minimum set of permissions required to perform their function.
verification_steps: 1. Every permission granted is explicitly justified
2. No wildcard permissions
3. Permissions are scoped to minimal resource set
anti_patterns: Using service accounts with full access
Reusing service accounts across multiple services

seed_knowledge.title: All external inputs are untrusted
seed_knowledge.description: Every input received from outside the trust boundary is treated as malicious until proven valid. This includes inputs from internal services.
verification_steps: 1. All input validated against strict schema
2. No deserialization of untrusted input
3. All output properly escaped for context

seed_knowledge.title: No security by obscurity
seed_knowledge.description: Security controls must remain effective even when full implementation details are publicly known.
verification_steps: 1. No secret algorithms or obfuscated security checks
2. All security controls use standard audited implementations
3. System security depends only on properly protected secrets

seed_knowledge.title: Security patches deployed within SLA
seed_knowledge.description: Critical security patches are deployed within 24 hours. High severity patches within 72 hours.
verification_steps: 1. Automated vulnerability scanning runs daily
2. Patch deployment pipeline has highest priority
3. All production instances report patch status
anti_patterns: Delaying security patches for feature releases

seed_knowledge.title: Fast feedback first
seed_knowledge.description: CI pipeline runs fastest validations first. Failures are reported as early as possible in the pipeline.
verification_steps: 1. Linting and static analysis run before compilation
2. Fast unit tests run before integration tests
3. Pipeline fails within 2 minutes for most common errors
anti_patterns: Running full test suite for every change
Waiting for full pipeline to report simple errors
Ordering pipeline steps by historical convention

seed_knowledge.title: Immutable build artifacts
seed_knowledge.description: Once built, artifacts are never modified. Exactly the same artifact is promoted through all environments.
verification_steps: 1. Build artifacts have immutable cryptographic hash
2. No rebuilds between environments
3. Environment configuration is injected at runtime

seed_knowledge.title: All changes go through pipeline
seed_knowledge.description: No direct changes to production. Every modification to production state must pass through full CI/CD pipeline.
verification_steps: 1. Production access is read only
2. All changes are tracked in version control
3. Audit log exists for every production change

seed_knowledge.title: Pipeline must be idempotent
seed_knowledge.description: Running the same pipeline multiple times with same input produces exactly the same result.
verification_steps: 1. No timestamp based build metadata
2. All dependencies are pinned to exact versions
3. Re-running pipeline produces identical artifact hash

seed_knowledge.title: Rollback is first response
seed_knowledge.description: For any production incident, first action is to rollback to last known good version. Debugging happens after service is restored.
verification_steps: 1. One click automated rollback available
2. Rollback completes within 5 minutes
3. No debugging attempted before rollback

seed_knowledge.title: Measure before optimizing
seed_knowledge.description: Never optimize code before collecting empirical performance data that identifies the exact bottleneck.
verification_steps: 1. Capture profiling data under realistic load
2. Identify top 1% of code consuming 90% of resources
3. Verify improvement with same profiling after change
anti_patterns: Optimizing code that is not on hot path

seed_knowledge.title: No blocking operations on critical path
seed_knowledge.description: All blocking IO operations must be asynchronous or offloaded from request processing threads.
verification_steps: 1. No synchronous IO calls on request thread
2. All external calls have explicit timeouts
3. Thread pool size matches IO concurrency requirements
anti_patterns: Synchronous database calls on request path

seed_knowledge.title: Cache invalidation explicit
seed_knowledge.description: Every cached value has explicit TTL and explicit invalidation trigger. No permanent cache entries.
verification_steps: 1. Every cache entry has maximum TTL defined
2. Modification operations invalidate corresponding cache entries
3. Cache miss handling is always tested
anti_patterns: Assuming cache will always be present

seed_knowledge.title: Degrade gracefully under load
seed_knowledge.description: System must continue operating with reduced functionality instead of completely failing under excess load.
verification_steps: 1. Load shedding implemented at entry point
2. Non critical features are disabled first under load
3. System recovers automatically after load reduces

seed_knowledge.title: All external calls have timeouts
seed_knowledge.description: Every outgoing network call has explicit, reasonable timeout value set. No infinite timeouts allowed.
verification_steps: 1. Every network call defines connect and read timeouts
2. Timeout values are based on observed latency percentiles
3. Timeout failures are properly handled

seed_knowledge.title: All operations have quotas
seed_knowledge.description: Every user facing operation has explicit rate limit and quota defined. No unlimited operations.
verification_steps: 1. Every API endpoint has rate limit configured
2. Quotas are enforced at entry point
3. Quota limits are published to users
anti_patterns: Quota enforcement only at application layer
Secret quota limits not communicated to users

seed_knowledge.title: Quotas are per identity
seed_knowledge.description: All quotas and rate limits are applied per authenticated identity. No global quotas for unauthenticated users.
verification_steps: 1. Unauthenticated requests have separate strict quotas
2. No user can consume more than their individual quota
3. Abusive users are isolated from other users
anti_patterns: Global rate limits that affect all users
No separate limits for unauthenticated access

seed_knowledge.title: Quota limits are defensive
seed_knowledge.description: Quota limits are set well below actual system capacity. Users should hit quota limits before system becomes overloaded.
verification_steps: 1. Quota limits set at 50% of measured maximum capacity
2. System remains healthy even when all users are at maximum quota
3. Quota increases require load testing verification
anti_patterns: Setting quotas at maximum system capacity
Allowing users to drive system to overload

seed_knowledge.title: Quota violations are logged
seed_knowledge.description: Every quota violation is logged with full context for abuse detection and analysis.
verification_steps: 1. All rate limit hits are logged with user identity
2. Repeated quota violations trigger alerts
3. Abuse patterns are automatically detected

seed_knowledge.title: Retry after header required
seed_knowledge.description: All quota rejection responses include standard Retry-After header with accurate wait time.
verification_steps: 1. Every 429 response includes Retry-After header
2. Header value indicates actual available time
3. Consistent header format across all endpoints

seed_knowledge.title: Blameless postmortem required
seed_knowledge.description: Every production incident requires postmortem with no individual blame. Focus is on systemic improvements.
verification_steps: 1. Postmortem completed within 3 business days
2. Document timeline, root cause and contributing factors
3. Action items are created and tracked

seed_knowledge.title: Five whys root cause analysis
seed_knowledge.description: For every incident, ask why at least 5 times until fundamental systemic root cause is identified.
verification_steps: 1. Stop at process or system level cause
2. Do not accept human error as root cause
3. Identify prevention measures for root cause

seed_knowledge.title: Every incident produces test cases
seed_knowledge.description: For every incident, create automated test case that would have detected the issue before production.
verification_steps: 1. Test case reproduces exact incident conditions
2. Test case added to standard test suite
3. Test case runs on every future commit
anti_patterns: Test case that does not reproduce actual issue

seed_knowledge.title: Incident metrics tracked over time
seed_knowledge.description: Incident frequency, duration and impact are tracked over time to measure improvement.
verification_steps: 1. All incidents are categorized and scored
2. Trends are reviewed monthly
3. Improvement targets are set
anti_patterns: Measuring only individual incident response time

seed_knowledge.title: Near misses are treated as incidents
seed_knowledge.description: Events that could have caused an outage but did not are analyzed with same rigor as actual incidents.
verification_steps: 1. Near misses are reported and documented
2. Full root cause analysis performed
3. Same improvement process followed

seed_knowledge.title: All production states are observable
seed_knowledge.description: Every internal state of production system is exposed via metrics, logs or traces. No hidden state.
verification_steps: 1. Every subsystem has health metrics
2. Logs allow reconstruction of any event
3. No production state is only visible to debugger
anti_patterns: State that cannot be observed remotely

seed_knowledge.title: Alerts are actionable only
seed_knowledge.description: Every alert requires immediate human action. Alerts that do not require action are removed.
verification_steps: 1. Every alert has documented response procedure
2. No alerts that can be safely ignored
3. Alert threshold set to trigger before user impact
anti_patterns: Noisy alerts that are routinely ignored

seed_knowledge.title: Single pane of glass observability
seed_knowledge.description: All operational data is available in single unified interface. No need to check multiple systems during incident.
verification_steps: 1. Metrics, logs and traces are correlated
2. Single dashboard for each service
3. All context available within 1 click
anti_patterns: Operational data scattered across multiple tools

seed_knowledge.title: Backup restoration tested regularly
seed_knowledge.description: Every backup system has restoration tested on regular schedule. Backups are only valid if they can be restored.
verification_steps: 1. Full restoration test performed monthly
2. Restoration time measured and documented
3. Test restoration data validated for correctness

seed_knowledge.title: Change freeze before holidays
seed_knowledge.description: No production changes are deployed 24 hours before weekends or public holidays. Only critical security fixes allowed.
verification_steps: 1. Change freeze period automatically enforced
2. Exceptions require senior approval
3. All changes completed before freeze begins

seed_knowledge.title: All API operations idempotent
seed_knowledge.description: Every API operation must produce same result regardless of how many times it is called.
verification_steps: 1. All write operations require client generated idempotency key
2. Retrying operation has no additional side effects
3. Same response returned for duplicate requests
anti_patterns: Operations that create duplicates on retry
No idempotency protection for write operations

seed_knowledge.title: API versioned from first release
seed_knowledge.description: All public APIs include explicit version identifier from first release. Breaking changes require new version.
verification_steps: 1. Version identifier present in every request
2. Old versions supported until fully deprecated
3. Breaking changes never made to existing version
anti_patterns: Adding versioning after breaking changes are needed

seed_knowledge.title: Circuit breaker for all external calls
seed_knowledge.description: Every external service call is wrapped with circuit breaker that stops sending requests when failure rate exceeds threshold.
verification_steps: 1. Circuit breaker enabled for all external calls
2. Failure threshold and recovery time configured
3. Fallback behavior defined for open circuit state
anti_patterns: Continuously sending requests to failed service

seed_knowledge.title: Response schema always consistent
seed_knowledge.description: API always returns exactly same response schema for both success and error cases. No variable schema.
verification_steps: 1. All responses follow same envelope format
2. Error responses include same base fields as success
3. No optional fields that disappear in error cases

seed_knowledge.title: Health check endpoints standardized
seed_knowledge.description: All services implement standard health check endpoint that returns overall status and individual component health.
verification_steps: 1. /health endpoint returns 200 when healthy
2. Health check verifies all critical dependencies
3. Response includes machine readable status codes
anti_patterns: Health checks that test non critical dependencies

seed_knowledge.title: Request tracing end to end
seed_knowledge.description: Every request receives unique trace ID that is propagated across all service calls and included in all log entries.
verification_steps: 1. Trace ID generated at entry point
2. Trace ID passed to all downstream services
3. Every log entry includes active trace ID
anti_patterns: Trace ID not included in logs
Generating new trace IDs per service

seed_knowledge.title: No internal APIs exposed publicly
seed_knowledge.description: Internal service APIs are never exposed to public internet. All public traffic goes through dedicated API gateway.
verification_steps: 1. Internal services have no public IP addresses
2. All public traffic passes through gateway with authentication
3. Internal API endpoints are not reachable from internet
anti_patterns: Exposing internal services directly to internet
Using same authentication for internal and public APIs

seed_knowledge.title: Graceful shutdown required
seed_knowledge.description: All services handle termination signal correctly, finish in flight requests and clean up resources before exiting.
verification_steps: 1. Service stops accepting new connections on termination signal
2. All in flight requests complete within timeout
3. Resources are properly released before exit
anti_patterns: Not cleaning up connections on shutdown

conflicts_with_rules: Any practice that requires hardcoding specific model names, provider names or vendor specific APIs directly conflicts with all seed knowledge
Any pattern that makes core functionality dependent on external AI services directly conflicts with solo-first capability rule
Any fix that modifies more code than absolutely required directly conflicts with smallest correct fix principle
Any security control that fails open directly conflicts with security first architectural principle
Any state stored in process memory directly conflicts with cloud-first stateless architecture
priority_list: SK-0018: All external inputs are untrusted
SK-0030: All external calls have timeouts
SK-0023: All changes go through pipeline
